# Tack

> Tack is a Go-based configuration management and system bootstrapping tool.
> It is NOT Ansible. Do NOT use Ansible syntax. Read the RULES section carefully.

Version: 1.0
Repository: https://github.com/tackhq/tack

Tack manages system state through declarative YAML playbooks executed via SSH,
AWS SSM, Docker, or locally. It supports idempotent modules for packages, files,
templates, services, and commands. Written in pure Go with no CGo dependencies.

---

## RULES

These rules override any Ansible knowledge. Tack looks similar but differs in
critical ways.

1. Use `sudo: true`, NOT `become: true`. Tack has no `become` keyword.
2. Templates use Go `text/template` syntax: `{{ .var }}`, NOT Jinja2 `{{ var }}`.
3. Playbook variable interpolation uses `{{ var }}`, NOT `{{ .var }}`.
   - In YAML playbooks: `{{ my_var }}` (no dot prefix)
   - In .tmpl template files: `{{ .my_var }}` (dot prefix, Go template syntax)
4. There is no `apt_repository`, `pip`, `docker_container`,
   `service`, `uri`, or `shell`
   module. Only the modules listed below exist.
5. There is no `become_user`, `become_method`, `environment`, `timeout`,
   `delegate_to`, `run_once`, `serial`, `strategy`, `any_errors_fatal`,
   `max_fail_percentage`, or `collections` directive.
6. There is no `with_items`. Use `loop:` instead.
7. There is no `template` lookup or `lookup()` function.
8. Handlers use the same module syntax as tasks. There is no `listen` directive.
9. Filters use `{{ var | filter(arg) }}` syntax. Only the filters listed in
   the FILTERS section exist. There is no `regex_replace`, `map`, `select`,
   `reject`, `dict2items`, `combine`, or `to_json`/`from_json` filter.
10. Inventory can be a static YAML file, an executable script (auto-detected),
    or a plugin config file (plugin: http, plugin: ec2). Not INI format.
11. Roles follow the same directory layout as Ansible roles but only support:
    tasks/, handlers/, vars/, defaults/, files/, templates/.
    There is no meta/, library/, module_utils/, or lookup_plugins/.
12. Variable files use `vars_files:` (list of YAML paths). Prefix with `?` for
    optional files: `?optional_vars.yaml`.
13. Encrypted secrets use `vault_file:` with AES-256-GCM, not `ansible-vault`.

---

## PLAYBOOK SCHEMA

### Play-level fields

| Field          | Type              | Default   | Description                              |
|----------------|-------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------|
| name           | string            | ""        | Optional play description                |
| hosts          | []string          | -         | Target host patterns                     |
| connection     | string            | "local"   | Connection type: local, ssh, ssm, docker |
| gather_facts   | bool              | true      | Gather system facts before tasks         |
| sudo           | bool              | false     | Enable privilege escalation              |
| sudo_password  | string            | ""        | Password for sudo                        |
| vars           | map[string]any    | {}        | Play-level variables                     |
| vars_files     | []string          | []        | YAML files to load as variables          |
| vault_file     | string            | ""        | Encrypted vault file path                |
| tags           | string or []string| []        | Play-level tags inherited by all tasks   |
| roles          | []string or []map | []        | Roles to include (string or {role:, tags:}) |
| tasks          | []Task            | []        | Tasks to execute                         |
| handlers       | []Task            | []        | Handlers triggered by notify             |
| ssh            | SSHConfig         | null      | SSH connection settings                  |
| ssm            | SSMConfig         | null      | AWS SSM connection settings              |

### Task-level fields

| Field          | Type              | Default   | Description                              |
|----------------|-------------------|-----------|------------------------------------------|
| name           | string            | ""        | Task description                         |
| module         | string            | -         | Module name (or use shorthand)           |
| params         | map[string]any    | {}        | Module parameters                        |
| when           | string            | ""        | Conditional expression                   |
| register       | string            | ""        | Store result in this variable            |
| notify         | []string          | []        | Handlers to trigger on change            |
| loop           | []any             | null      | Iterate task over list                   |
| loop_var       | string            | "item"    | Variable name for current loop item      |
| ignore_errors  | bool              | false     | Continue on failure                      |
| retries        | int               | 0         | Retry count on failure                   |
| delay          | int               | 0         | Seconds between retries                  |
| sudo           | *bool             | null      | Override play-level sudo (null=inherit)  |
| changed_when   | string            | ""        | Expression to override change detection  |
| failed_when    | string            | ""        | Expression to override failure detection |
| tags           | string or []string| []        | Tags for selective execution via --tags/--skip-tags |

Tags can also be set on plays, blocks, and role references. Tag inheritance: play > role > block > task (additive union). Special tags: `always` (runs even when --tags active), `never` (skipped unless explicitly --tags'd). Handlers ignore --tags but respect --skip-tags.

### Task shorthand syntax

Tasks can specify the module as a top-level key:

```yaml
# Shorthand (preferred)
- name: Install nginx
  apt:
    name: nginx
    state: present

# Explicit (also valid)
- name: Install nginx
  module: apt
  params:
    name: nginx
    state: present
```

### Playbook example

```yaml
- name: Configure web server
  hosts:
    - web-servers
  connection: ssh
  gather_facts: true
  sudo: true

  vars:
    app_port: 8080

  vars_files:
    - vars/common.yaml
    - ?vars/local.yaml

  tasks:
    - name: Install nginx
      apt:
        name: nginx
        state: present

    - name: Deploy config
      template:
        src: nginx.conf.tmpl
        dest: /etc/nginx/sites-available/default
      notify:
        - Restart nginx

    - name: Ensure nginx running
      systemd:
        name: nginx
        state: started
        enabled: true

  handlers:
    - name: Restart nginx
      systemd:
        name: nginx
        state: restarted
```

---

## MODULES

Do not hallucinate modules beyond this list.

### apt

Manage packages on Debian/Ubuntu systems.

| Param              | Type           | Required | Default   | Description                          |
|--------------------|----------------|----------|-----------|--------------------------------------|
| name               | string/[]string| yes      | -         | Package name(s)                      |
| state              | string         | no       | "present" | present, absent, latest, purged      |
| update_cache       | bool           | no       | false     | Run apt-get update first             |
| upgrade            | string         | no       | "none"    | none, yes, safe, full, dist          |
| cache_valid_time   | int            | no       | 0         | Cache validity in seconds            |
| install_recommends | bool           | no       | true      | Install recommended packages         |
| autoremove         | bool           | no       | false     | Remove unused dependencies           |
| deb                | string         | no       | ""        | Path or URL to .deb file             |

```yaml
- name: Install packages
  apt:
    name:
      - nginx
      - curl
    state: present
    update_cache: true
```

### brew

Manage Homebrew packages on macOS.

| Param           | Type           | Required | Default   | Description                     |
|-----------------|----------------|----------|-----------|---------------------------------|
| name            | string/[]string| yes      | -         | Package name(s)                 |
| state           | string         | no       | "present" | present, absent, latest         |
| cask            | bool           | no       | false     | Install as cask (GUI app)       |
| upgrade_all     | bool           | no       | false     | Upgrade all installed packages  |
| update_homebrew | bool           | no       | false     | Run brew update first           |
| options         | []string       | no       | []        | Extra flags for brew install    |

```yaml
- name: Install VS Code
  brew:
    name: visual-studio-code
    cask: true
    state: present
```

### yum

Manage packages on RPM-based systems (RHEL, CentOS, Fedora, Rocky).
Automatically uses dnf if available.

| Param        | Type           | Required | Default   | Description                     |
|--------------|----------------|----------|-----------|---------------------------------|
| name         | string/[]string| yes      | -         | Package name(s)                 |
| state        | string         | no       | "present" | present, absent, latest         |
| update_cache | bool           | no       | false     | Run yum makecache first         |
| upgrade      | string         | no       | "none"    | none, yes                       |
| autoremove   | bool           | no       | false     | Remove unused dependencies      |

```yaml
- name: Install httpd
  yum:
    name: httpd
    state: latest
    update_cache: true
```

### command

Execute shell commands. Always reports changed unless skipped by creates/removes.

| Param    | Type   | Required | Default | Description                              |
|----------|--------|----------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| cmd      | string | yes      | -       | Command to execute                       |
| chdir    | string | no       | ""      | Working directory                        |
| creates  | string | no       | ""      | Skip if this path exists (idempotency)   |
| removes  | string | no       | ""      | Only run if this path exists             |
| warn     | bool   | no       | true    | Warn about common issues                 |

Result data: `stdout`, `stderr`, `exit_code`

```yaml
- name: Initialize database
  command:
    cmd: /usr/local/bin/initdb --data-dir /var/lib/db
    creates: /var/lib/db/initialized
```

### copy

Copy files or inline content to targets.

| Param       | Type   | Required | Default | Description                              |
|-------------|--------|----------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| dest        | string | yes      | -       | Destination path                         |
| src         | string | no*      | ""      | Source file path (*or use content)        |
| content     | string | no*      | ""      | Inline content (*or use src)             |
| mode        | string | no       | "0644"  | File permissions (octal)                 |
| owner       | string | no       | ""      | File owner                               |
| group       | string | no       | ""      | File group                               |
| backup      | bool   | no       | false   | Backup before overwriting                |
| force       | bool   | no       | true    | Overwrite if destination exists          |
| create_dirs | bool   | no       | false   | Create parent directories                |
| validate    | string | no       | ""      | Validation command (%s = temp file path) |

Either `src` or `content` must be specified (mutually exclusive).
When used in a role, `src` paths resolve relative to the role's `files/` directory.

```yaml
- name: Deploy app config
  copy:
    src: app.conf
    dest: /etc/myapp/app.conf
    mode: "0640"
    owner: myapp
    backup: true
```

### file

Manage files, directories, and symlinks.

| Param   | Type   | Required | Default | Description                              |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| path    | string | yes      | -       | Target path                              |
| state   | string | no       | "file"  | file, directory, link, absent, touch     |
| mode    | string | no       | ""      | Permissions (octal, e.g. "0755")         |
| owner   | string | no       | ""      | Owner username                           |
| group   | string | no       | ""      | Group name                               |
| src     | string | no       | ""      | Link target (required for state=link)    |
| recurse | bool   | no       | false   | Recurse into directories                 |
| force   | bool   | no       | false   | Force symlink creation                   |

States:
- `file` - Ensure exists, set attributes (error if missing)
- `directory` - Create directory and set attributes
- `link` - Create symlink (requires `src`)
- `absent` - Remove file/directory
- `touch` - Create empty file or update timestamp

```yaml
- name: Create app directory
  file:
    path: /opt/myapp
    state: directory
    mode: "0755"
    owner: deploy
```

### systemd

Manage systemd services.

| Param         | Type  | Required | Default | Description                         |
|---------------|-------|----------|---------|-------------------------------------|
| name          | string| yes      | -       | Service unit name                   |
| state         | string| no       | ""      | started, stopped, restarted, reloaded |
| enabled       | *bool | no       | null    | Enable/disable at boot (null=no change) |
| daemon_reload | bool  | no       | false   | Run daemon-reload first             |
| masked        | *bool | no       | null    | Mask/unmask service (null=no change)|

Unit names get `.service` suffix automatically if no extension is present.
`started`/`stopped` are idempotent. `restarted`/`reloaded` always execute.

```yaml
- name: Enable and start Docker
  systemd:
    name: docker
    state: started
    enabled: true
    daemon_reload: true
```

### template

Render Go templates and deploy to targets.

| Param  | Type   | Required | Default | Description                              |
|--------|--------|----------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| src    | string | yes      | -       | Template file path (.tmpl)               |
| dest   | string | yes      | -       | Destination path on target               |
| mode   | string | no       | "0644"  | File permissions (octal)                 |
| owner  | string | no       | ""      | File owner                               |
| group  | string | no       | ""      | File group                               |
| backup | bool   | no       | false   | Backup before overwriting                |

Templates use Go `text/template` syntax (NOT Jinja2):
- Variables: `{{ .var_name }}`
- Conditionals: `{{ if .flag }}...{{ end }}`
- Ranges: `{{ range .items }}{{ . }}{{ end }}`

Available template functions: `default`, `lower`, `upper`, `trim`, `join`, `ssm_param`

When used in a role, `src` paths resolve relative to the role's `templates/` directory.

```yaml
- name: Deploy nginx config
  template:
    src: nginx.conf.tmpl
    dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    mode: "0644"
  notify:
    - Reload nginx
```

### wait_for

Wait for a condition to be met before proceeding. Polls until success or timeout.

| Param    | Type   | Required | Default     | Description                                    |
|----------|--------|----------|-------------|------------------------------------------------|
| type     | string | yes      | -           | port, path, command, or url                    |
| host     | string | no       | "localhost" | Host for port check                            |
| port     | int    | no       | -           | TCP port (required for type=port)              |
| path     | string | no       | -           | File path (required for type=path)             |
| cmd      | string | no       | -           | Shell command (required for type=command)       |
| url      | string | no       | -           | HTTP(S) URL (required for type=url)            |
| timeout  | int    | no       | 300         | Max wait in seconds                            |
| interval | int    | no       | 5           | Poll interval in seconds                       |
| state    | string | no       | "started"   | started or stopped (port and path types only)  |

Port and URL checks run from the controller. Path and command checks run on the target via the connector.

Result data: `elapsed`, `attempts`, plus `stdout`/`stderr` (command) or `status_code` (url).

```yaml
- name: Wait for app to start
  wait_for:
    type: port
    port: 8080
    timeout: 60

- name: Wait for health endpoint
  wait_for:
    type: url
    url: http://localhost:8080/health
    timeout: 120
    interval: 5

- name: Wait for database
  wait_for:
    type: command
    cmd: pg_isready -h localhost
    timeout: 30

- name: Wait for lock file removal
  wait_for:
    type: path
    path: /var/lock/deploy.lock
    state: stopped
```

### assert

Built-in task keyword (not a registered module). Validates preconditions on the control host using the same condition engine as `when:`. Never invokes the connector.

| Param       | Type              | Required | Default | Description                                 |
|-------------|-------------------|----------|---------|---------------------------------------------|
| that        | string / []string | yes      | -       | One or more boolean expressions             |
| fail_msg    | string            | no       | -       | Custom message on failure                   |
| success_msg | string            | no       | -       | Message when all conditions pass            |
| quiet       | bool              | no       | false   | Suppress per-condition output on success    |

Operators: same as `when:` — `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `in`, `not in`, `is defined`, `is not defined`, `and`, `or`, `not`, parens.

Works with `when:`, `tags:`, `register:`, `block:`/`rescue:`/`always:`, and `--dry-run` (assert failures fail the play in dry-run too). `--diff` is a no-op. Task always reports `changed: false`.

```yaml
- name: preflight
  assert:
    that:
      - "facts.os_type == 'Linux'"
      - "deploy_env in ['staging', 'prod']"
    fail_msg: "unsupported environment"
```

Registered result shape:
```
{changed: false, failed: bool, msg: string,
 evaluated_conditions: [{expr: "...", result: true|false}, ...]}
```

### lineinfile

Ensure a specific line is present or absent in a file.

| Param        | Type   | Required | Default   | Description                                    |
|--------------|--------|----------|-----------|------------------------------------------------|
| path         | string | yes      | -         | Path to the target file                        |
| line         | string | no*      | -         | Line to ensure (*required when state=present)  |
| regexp       | string | no       | ""        | Regex to match lines for replacement/removal   |
| state        | string | no       | "present" | present, absent                                |
| insertafter  | string | no       | "EOF"     | Insert after this regex match or EOF           |
| insertbefore | string | no       | ""        | Insert before this regex match or BOF          |
| create       | bool   | no       | false     | Create file if it does not exist               |
| backup       | bool   | no       | false     | Create backup before modifying                 |

```yaml
- name: Ensure sysctl setting
  lineinfile:
    path: /etc/sysctl.conf
    regexp: "^net.ipv4.ip_forward"
    line: "net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1"
```

### blockinfile

Manage a block of text between marker lines in a file.

| Param        | Type   | Required | Default                       | Description                          |
|--------------|--------|----------|-------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| path         | string | yes      | -                             | Path to the target file              |
| block        | string | no       | ""                            | Multi-line content between markers   |
| marker       | string | no       | "# {mark} BOLT MANAGED BLOCK"| Marker format with {mark} placeholder|
| marker_begin | string | no       | "BEGIN"                       | Text for {mark} in begin marker      |
| marker_end   | string | no       | "END"                         | Text for {mark} in end marker        |
| state        | string | no       | "present"                     | present, absent                      |
| insertafter  | string | no       | ""                            | Insert after this regex or EOF       |
| insertbefore | string | no       | ""                            | Insert before this regex or BOF      |
| create       | bool   | no       | false                         | Create file if it does not exist     |
| backup       | bool   | no       | false                         | Create backup before modifying       |

```yaml
- name: Add SSH config block
  blockinfile:
    path: /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    block: |
      Match Group sftp
        ForceCommand internal-sftp
        ChrootDirectory /home/%u
```

### user

Manage system users on Linux using useradd/usermod/userdel.

| Param    | Type           | Required | Default   | Description                                  |
|----------|----------------|----------|-----------|----------------------------------------------|
| name     | string         | yes      | -         | Username                                     |
| state    | string         | no       | "present" | present, absent                              |
| uid      | int            | no       | -         | User ID                                      |
| shell    | string         | no       | ""        | Login shell (e.g., /bin/bash)                |
| home     | string         | no       | ""        | Home directory path                          |
| groups   | []string       | no       | []        | Supplementary groups (appended)              |
| system   | bool           | no       | false     | Create a system user                         |
| password | string         | no       | ""        | Pre-hashed password (SHA-512 crypt format)   |
| remove   | bool           | no       | false     | Remove home dir when state=absent            |

Linux only. Passwords must be pre-hashed. Groups are appended via `usermod -aG`.

```yaml
- name: Create deploy user
  user:
    name: deploy
    shell: /bin/bash
    groups:
      - docker
      - wheel

- name: Remove old user
  user:
    name: olduser
    state: absent
    remove: true
```

### group

Manage system groups on Linux using groupadd/groupmod/groupdel.

| Param  | Type   | Required | Default   | Description              |
|--------|--------|----------|-----------|--------------------------|
| name   | string | yes      | -         | Group name               |
| state  | string | no       | "present" | present, absent          |
| gid    | int    | no       | -         | Group ID                 |
| system | bool   | no       | false     | Create a system group    |

Linux only.

```yaml
- name: Create deploy group
  group:
    name: deploy
    gid: 1500

- name: Remove old group
  group:
    name: oldgroup
    state: absent
```

### git

Check out a git repository to the target idempotently. Compares current HEAD
to the resolved target SHA and skips fetch/checkout when they match.
Requires the `git` binary on the target. SSH auth only in v1.

| Param          | Type   | Required | Default | Description                                       |
|----------------|--------|----------|---------|---------------------------------------------------|
| repo           | string | yes      | -       | Repository URL (SSH or HTTPS)                     |
| dest           | string | yes      | -       | Absolute path on target                           |
| version        | string | no       | -       | Branch, tag, or SHA; defaults to remote HEAD      |
| force          | bool   | no       | false   | Reset dirty worktree before checkout              |
| depth          | int    | no       | 0       | Shallow clone depth (0 = full)                    |
| update         | bool   | no       | true    | Fetch+checkout when repo already exists           |
| clone          | bool   | no       | true    | Clone when dest is missing                        |
| bare           | bool   | no       | false   | Create bare clone (no worktree)                   |
| single_branch  | bool   | no       | false   | Clone only the target branch                      |
| recursive      | bool   | no       | false   | Init/update submodules after checkout             |
| accept_hostkey | bool   | no       | false   | StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new (TOFU)           |
| key_file       | string | no       | ""      | Path on target to SSH key file                    |

Registered result fields: `changed`, `before_sha`, `after_sha`, `remote_url`,
`version_resolved`, `warnings` (list).

```yaml
- name: Deploy app code
  git:
    repo: git@github.com:acme/app.git
    dest: /opt/app
    version: v2.4.1
  register: repo

- name: Tag deploy with the checked-out SHA
  command:
    cmd: "echo deployed {{ repo.after_sha }}"
```

---

## CLI

### Commands

| Command                | Description                                  |
|------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| `tack run <target>`    | Execute a playbook or role                   |
| `tack validate <file>` | Validate playbook syntax without executing   |
| `tack modules`         | List available modules                       |
| `tack module <name>`   | Show module documentation                    |
| `tack test <target>`   | Test in ephemeral Docker container           |
| `tack scaffold <name>` | Generate sample role directory structure      |
| `tack generate`        | Capture live system state as a playbook      |
| `tack vault init <f>`  | Create new encrypted vault file              |
| `tack vault edit <f>`  | Edit existing encrypted vault file           |
| `tack inventory`       | Inspect resolved inventory (--list, --host)  |
| `tack version`         | Show version information                     |

Target can be a playbook YAML file or a role directory path.

### Global flags

| Flag          | Short | Type   | Default | Description                    |
|---------------|-------|--------|---------|--------------------------------|
| --debug       | -d    | bool   | false   | Detailed task output           |
| --verbose     | -v    | bool   | false   | Show full diffs in plan output |
| --dry-run     | -n    | bool   | false   | Show what would change         |
| --check       |       | bool   | false   | Alias for --dry-run            |
| --no-color    |       | bool   | false   | Disable colored output         |
| --output      |       | string | "text"  | Output format: text, json      |

### Run flags

| Flag                 | Short | Type   | Default | Env var              |
|----------------------|-------|--------|---------|----------------------|
| --connection         | -c    | string | ""      | TACK_CONNECTION      |
| --hosts              |       | string | ""      | TACK_HOSTS           |
| --sudo               | -s    | bool   | false   | -                    |
| --sudo-password      |       | string | ""      | TACK_SUDO_PASSWORD   |
| --inventory          | -i    | string | ""      | -  (repeatable)      |
| --inventory-timeout  |       | int    | 30      | -                    |
| --extra-vars         | -e    | string | ""      | -                    |
| --tags               |       | string | ""      | -                    |
| --skip-tags          |       | string | ""      | -                    |
| --forks              | -f    | int    | 1       | TACK_FORKS           |
| --auto-approve       |       | bool   | false   | -                    |
| --ssh-user           |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSH_USER        |
| --ssh-port           |       | int    | 0       | TACK_SSH_PORT        |
| --ssh-key            |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSH_KEY         |
| --ssh-password       |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSH_PASSWORD    |
| --ssh-insecure       |       | bool   | false   | TACK_SSH_INSECURE    |
| --vault-password-file|       | string | ""      | -                    |
| --ssm-instances      |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSM_INSTANCES   |
| --ssm-tags           |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSM_TAGS        |
| --ssm-region         |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSM_REGION      |
| --ssm-bucket         |       | string | ""      | TACK_SSM_BUCKET      |

`--connection` accepts URI format: `ssh://user@host:port`, `docker://container`, `local://`
`--extra-vars` accepts `key=value` format, repeatable.
`--vault-password-file` reads the first line of the file as the password.
Vault password resolution: `TACK_VAULT_PASSWORD` env > `--vault-password-file` > interactive prompt.

### Test flags

| Flag    | Type   | Default        | Description                    |
|---------|--------|----------------|--------------------------------|
| --image |string  | "ubuntu:24.04" | Docker image for testing       |
| --new   | bool   | false          | Force fresh container          |
| --rm    | bool   | false          | Remove container after test    |

### Generate flags

| Flag       | Type   | Description                     |
|------------|--------|---------------------------------|
| --packages | string | Packages to capture (comma-sep) |
| --files    | string | Files/dirs to capture           |
| --services | string | Systemd services to capture     |
| --users    | string | Users to capture                |
| --output   | string | Output file (default: stdout)   |

### Scaffold flags

| Flag   | Type   | Default | Description               |
|--------|--------|---------|---------------------------|
| --path | string | "roles" | Base directory for role    |

---

## CONNECTORS

### local

Executes commands on the local machine. Default connection type.

```yaml
connection: local
```

No additional configuration needed.

### ssh

Connects to remote hosts via SSH. Reads `~/.ssh/config` and `~/.ssh/known_hosts`.

```yaml
connection: ssh
ssh:
  user: deploy
  port: 22
  key: ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  password: ""
  host_key_checking: true
```

Authentication priority: explicit key > SSH agent > default keys (~/.ssh/id_ed25519, id_rsa, id_ecdsa) > password.

File transfers use SFTP. When sudo is enabled and user is not root, files upload
to /tmp first then move via sudo.

### ssm

Connects to EC2 instances via AWS Systems Manager.

```yaml
connection: ssm
ssm:
  region: us-east-1
  bucket: my-transfer-bucket
  instances:
    - i-0123456789abcdef0
  tags:
    Environment: production
```

Requires SSM agent on instances and IAM permissions (ssm:SendCommand,
ssm:GetCommandInvocation). File transfers >24KB require an S3 bucket.

Instance targeting: `instances` for explicit IDs, `tags` for dynamic discovery
via EC2 API. Uses standard AWS credential chain.

### docker

Executes commands inside Docker containers.

```yaml
connection: docker
```

Connect via CLI: `tack run playbook.yaml -c docker://container-name`

Uses `docker exec` for commands and `docker cp` for file transfers.

---

## VARIABLES

### Interpolation syntax

```yaml
# Simple variable
name: "{{ app_name }}"

# Dotted path
family: "{{ facts.os_family }}"

# With filter
name: "{{ app_name | upper }}"
name: "{{ app_name | default('myapp') }}"

# SSM Parameter Store
secret: "{{ ssm_param('/prod/db/password') }}"
secret: "{{ ssm_param(param_path_var) }}"
```

Variable interpolation uses `{{ var }}` syntax (no dot prefix).
When the entire value is a single `{{ var }}` reference, the original type is
preserved (not stringified).

### Filters

| Filter          | Args     | Description                          |
|-----------------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| default(val)    | string   | Return val if variable is nil/empty  |
| lower           | -        | Lowercase string                     |
| upper           | -        | Uppercase string                     |
| trim            | -        | Strip whitespace                     |
| bool            | -        | Convert to boolean                   |
| string          | -        | Convert to string                    |
| int             | -        | Convert to integer                   |
| first           | -        | First element of list                |
| last            | -        | Last element of list                 |
| length / count  | -        | Length of string, list, or map       |
| join(sep)       | string   | Join list with separator (default ",") |

These are the ONLY filters. No others exist.

### Variable precedence (highest to lowest)

1. Registered task results (`register:`)
2. Play vars (`vars:` block)
3. Facts (gathered at play start)
4. CLI extra vars (`-e key=value`)
5. Vault variables (`vault_file:`)

### when: conditions

```yaml
when: facts.os_family == 'Debian'
when: app_port > 1024 and app_port < 65535
when: item in ['web', 'api', 'worker']
when: custom_var is defined
when: custom_var is not defined
when: not skip_deploy
when: (env == 'prod') or (force_deploy == true)
```

Operators: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `and`, `or`, `not`, `in`, `not in`,
`is defined`, `is not defined`. Parentheses for grouping.

Truthiness: non-empty strings, non-zero numbers, and `true` are truthy.
Empty strings, zero, `nil`, and `false` are falsy.

---

## FACTS

Gathered automatically unless `gather_facts: false`. Available as `{{ key }}`
or `{{ facts.key }}` in playbook variables and `{{ .key }}` in templates.

For multi-host plays, fact gathering runs concurrently across all hosts
regardless of `--forks`. The pre-pass is skipped for single-host plays,
`connection: local`, and `gather_facts: false`. Internal concurrency cap: 20.

Multi-host plays also render a single consolidated plan with per-line host
prefix (`web1: + apt: install nginx`) and prompt for approval once globally.
Hosts with only no-op tasks are omitted from the body and counted in the
footer as `(N unchanged)`. Single-host plays use the original per-task plan
format unchanged.

### System

| Fact          | Example           | Description                |
|---------------|-------------------|----------------------------|
| os_type       | "Linux", "Darwin" | OS from uname              |
| os_family     | "Debian", "RedHat", "Darwin" | Derived OS family |
| os_name       | "Ubuntu", "macOS" | OS name                    |
| os_version    | "14.5"            | macOS version              |
| distribution  | "ubuntu", "rhel"  | Linux distro ID            |
| distribution_version | "22.04"    | Linux distro version       |
| architecture  | "x86_64", "aarch64" | CPU architecture (raw)   |
| arch          | "amd64", "arm64"  | Normalized architecture    |
| kernel        | "6.1.0"           | Kernel version             |
| hostname      | "web-01"          | System hostname            |
| user          | "deploy"          | Current user               |
| home          | "/home/deploy"    | Home directory             |
| pkg_manager   | "apt", "dnf", "brew" | Detected package manager|

### Network

| Fact              | Example          | Description               |
|-------------------|------------------|---------------------------|
| default_interface | "eth0", "en0"    | Default network interface |
| default_ipv4      | "10.0.1.50"      | Default IPv4 address      |
| all_ipv4          | "10.0.1.50,..."  | All non-localhost IPv4    |
| all_ipv6          | "2001:db8::1,..." | All global IPv6          |

### Environment

| Fact        | Example          | Description                |
|-------------|------------------|----------------------------|
| env.PATH    | "/usr/bin:..."   | PATH variable              |
| env.SHELL   | "/bin/bash"      | User shell                 |
| env.LANG    | "en_US.UTF-8"   | Locale                     |
| env.TERM    | "xterm-256color" | Terminal type              |
| env.EDITOR  | "vim"            | Default editor             |

### EC2 (only on AWS instances with IMDSv2)

| Fact              | Example              | Description            |
|-------------------|----------------------|------------------------|
| ec2_instance_id   | "i-0123456789abcdef0"| Instance ID            |
| ec2_region        | "us-east-1"         | AWS region             |
| ec2_az            | "us-east-1a"        | Availability zone      |
| ec2_instance_type | "t3.micro"          | Instance type          |
| ec2_ami_id        | "ami-0abcdef1234"   | AMI ID                 |
| ec2_private_ip    | "10.0.1.50"         | Private IPv4           |
| ec2_public_ip     | "54.123.45.67"      | Public IPv4            |

---

## ROLES

Directory structure:

```
roles/myrole/
  tasks/main.yaml       # Task list (required)
  handlers/main.yaml    # Handler list
  vars/main.yaml        # High-priority variables
  defaults/main.yaml    # Low-priority defaults
  files/                # Static files for copy module
  templates/            # Template files for template module
```

Usage:

```yaml
roles:
  - myrole
  - /absolute/path/to/role
```

Variable precedence: role vars/ > play vars > role defaults/

Run a role directly: `tack run roles/myrole`

---

## INVENTORY

Inventory defines hosts, groups, connection settings, and per-host variables.
Tack supports three inventory source types.

### Static YAML

```yaml
# hosts.yml
hosts:
  web1:
    ssh: { user: deploy, key: ~/.ssh/id_deploy }
    vars: { region: us-east-1 }
groups:
  webservers:
    hosts: [web1, web2]
    connection: ssh
    vars: { app_port: 8080 }
```

### Script (executable)

Any executable file passed via `-i` is auto-detected and run with `--list`.
Output must be JSON or YAML in Tack inventory format (same schema as static YAML).

```bash
tack run deploy.yaml -i ./my-inventory.sh    # auto-detected as executable
tack inventory --list -i ./my-inventory.sh    # inspect output
```

Scripts receive `--list` as the sole argument. Stdout is parsed; stderr is
captured for error messages. Non-zero exit is an error.

### Plugin configs

YAML files with a `plugin:` key dispatch to a built-in plugin.

**HTTP plugin** — fetch from REST API:
```yaml
plugin: http
url: https://cmdb.example.com/api/inventory
headers:
  Authorization: "Bearer {{ env.CMDB_TOKEN }}"
params:
  env: production
timeout: 10
tls:
  ca_cert: /etc/ssl/custom-ca.pem
  insecure_skip_verify: false
auth:
  basic:
    username: "{{ env.USER }}"
    password: "{{ env.PASS }}"
```

**EC2 plugin** — discover AWS instances by tags:
```yaml
plugin: ec2
regions: [us-east-1, us-west-2]
filters:
  tag:env: production
group_by: [tag:role, tag:env]
host_key: private_ip   # private_ip (default, ssh), public_ip (ssh), instance_id (ssm)
```

Auto-groups by tag values: `tag_role_worker`, `tag_env_production`. All instance
tags become host vars. Uses standard AWS credential chain.

### Multiple sources

Multiple `-i` flags merge inventories in order. Later sources override scalar
values; group host lists are unioned (deduplicated).

```bash
tack run deploy.yaml -i ec2.yml -i overrides.yml
```

### Inspect inventory

```bash
tack inventory --list -i hosts.yml           # dump full inventory as JSON
tack inventory --host web1 -i hosts.yml      # single host detail with group memberships
```

### Inventory flags

| Flag                 | Short | Default | Description                              |
|----------------------|-------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| --inventory          | -i    | ""      | Inventory source (repeatable)            |
| --inventory-timeout  |       | 30      | Plugin execution timeout in seconds      |

Config values in plugin YAML support `{{ env.VAR_NAME }}` interpolation.

---

## VAULT

Encrypted variable files using AES-256-GCM.

```bash
# Create new vault
tack vault init secrets.yaml

# Edit existing vault
tack vault edit secrets.yaml

# Use in playbook
```

```yaml
vault_file: secrets.yaml
tasks:
  - name: Use secret
    command:
      cmd: echo "{{ db_password }}"
```

Password resolution: `TACK_VAULT_PASSWORD` env var > `--vault-password-file` > interactive prompt.
